title: Preparation and Properties of Geminal and Vicinal Dihalides

concepts.title: By Addition of Alkyl Halides to Alkenes
concepts.conceptBody:
By Addition of $\mathbf{H-X}$ to Alkenes

Alkyl chlorides, bromides and iodides can be prepared by treating an alkene with corresponding hydrogen halide ($\text{HCl},\text{HBr}$ or $\text{HI}$). The addition of these compounds to alkene takes place according to Markownikov’s rule. The reaction proceeds by electrophilic addition of ${{\text{H}}^{+}}$to give more stable carbocation followed by attack of ${{\text{X}}^{-}}$. Anti-Markownikov addition of $\text{HBr}$ can be achieved, if the reaction is carried out in presence of peroxides $\left( {{\text{H}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{2}} \right.$ or benzoyl peroxide or di-tert-butyl peroxide). Addition of $\text{HBr}$ to alkenes in the presence of peroxide follows free radical mechanism.

$\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}-\text{CH}=\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\xrightarrow[Peroxide]{HBr}\underset{\left( {{2}^{{}^\circ }}\text{ radical } \right)}{\mathop{\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}-\overset{\centerdot }{\mathop{C}}\,H-\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}-\text{Br}}}\,\xrightarrow[{}]{HBr}\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}-\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}-\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{Br}+\overset{\centerdot }{\mathop{\text{Br}}}\,$
